summary: glob-helper postinstall exfiltrates host/user/filesystem data over HTTP
details: The package ships a postinstall.js that runs unattended on `npm install`. It imports fs, os, and http; collects host identity via os.hostname() and os.userInfo() plus the home directory path via os.homedir(); reads files from the installer's filesystem via fs.existsSync / fs.readFileSync; and then issues an http.request to transmit the collected data. This is the canonical install-time host-info + filesystem exfiltration shape, executed silently during dependency installation in any consumer's environment. The package's advertised name ("glob-helper") provides no legitimate justification for reading user identity and local files at install time and sending them to a remote endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
